The men were convicted over alleged plan to stage coup and organise deadly demonstrations over fuel prices last year

Two Russian men have been found guilty by an Angolan court of terrorism and spying after being accused of planning a coup and helping to organise deadly demonstrations in the southern African country last year.

Lev Lakshtanov and Igor Ratchin were sentenced to eight and 11 years in jail respectively. They had denied the charges, claiming they were in Angola for cultural and business activities.

September exercise called off after Kenya raises concerns over funding and jurisdiction for criminal offences

The British army has been forced to cancel training for its troops in Kenya after the Kenyan government refused to ratify a bilateral defence agreement.

While the UK government wanted to renew the existing agreement, Kenyan lawmakers had sought changes to give local authorities greater powers to hold British soldiers accountable for any offences committed in the country.

UK resident was evacuated from Democratic Republic of the Congo where there is an outbreak of the disease

A humanitarian worker is being monitored at a specialist hospital in London after potential exposure to the Ebola virus.

The UK resident was medically evacuated from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) where the worker had been treating patients with the disease, the UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) said.

events.title: Healthy diet too expensive for one in three people globally, UN report finds
events.summary:
Global hunger declined for the third year in a row in 2025 but experts call for action to bring down costs of fruit, vegetables and dairy as report shows 2.69bn people can’t afford to eat well

One in three people cannot afford to eat a healthy diet, a new report has found, prompting calls for urgent action on food affordability.

There are 2.69 billion people unable to afford a diverse diet that meets their energy and nutrient needs, and it will take “immense” amounts of work to achieve the global target of zero hunger by 2030, the annual report from five UN agencies warned.

Incumbent Carlos Vila Nova hopes to defeat his former party and secure second term as independent

Voters in São Tomé and Príncipe go to the polls for a presidential election on Sunday as one of Africa’s least populous countries seeks to burnish its democratic credentials.

According to the National Election Commission, about 142,000 people are registered to vote in the tiny African state’s elections, approximately 15% of whom live in the diaspora.

events.title: Weather tracker: More than 900 wildfires rage across Canada
events.summary:
Fires remain out of control in Northwest Territories and Ontario, while Argentina endures record night-time heat

Recent hot, dry weather in Canada has triggered hundreds of wildfires across the country. As of Wednesday, more than 900 were burning, scorching about 3m hectares of land. While fires have broken out in several provinces, the greatest concern is in the Northwest Territories and Ontario, where several remain out of control.

Poor air quality has been reported across affected regions, with wildfire smoke carried by winds into the US and even across the Atlantic to northern Europe. The risk of further blazes remains high as North America enters the peak of fire season.

events.title: Mayor shot dead in Mexico months after surviving assassination attempt
events.summary:
Valentín Lavín Romero is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in 2024

A mayor was shot dead on Wednesday in the Mexican state of Morelos after having survived a previous assassination attempt earlier this year. He is the 14th mayor to be murdered since Claudia Sheinbaum became president in October 2024.

Valentín Lavín Romero, who was from the Green party, an ally of the ruling leftwing Morena party, was wounded at the end of January when hitmen shot him at a petrol station on a motorway.

A planned joint US-Canada ceremony on Friday celebrating the opening of the Gordie Howe International Bridge connecting Detroit and Windsor, Ontario, is no longer expected to take place after Donald Trump announced a 50% tariff on most Canadian goods.

In announcing the tariffs, the president accused Canada of unfairly discriminating against US automobiles, alcohol and dairy products, escalating tensions with one of the United States’ closest allies.

Nicaragua’s congress, controlled by the country’s long-serving president, Daniel Ortega, has announced a “work plan” to implement the authoritarian leader’s order that the country should no longer hold elections.

The UN and the US strongly condemned Sunday’s announcement by Ortega, who has been in power since 2007 and, after a controversial constitutional change last year, now governs alongside his wife, Rosario Murillo, as “co-president”.

events.title: Sperm whales change ‘vowel’ sounds when boats are nearby, scientists discover
events.summary:
Researchers listening off the Caribbean island of Dominica found variations in the mammals’ audible clicks when marine traffic was close

Sperm whales produce a specific pattern of clicking noises when they hear the noise of boats, researchers have found. The pattern is so distinct that the scientists who identified it said they can now use it to predict when ships are nearby.

Researchers from Project Ceti (the Cetacean Translation Initiative), a non-profit research organisation working to decode sperm whale communication, spent four years tuning in on the calls of 15 sperm whales off the coast of Dominica.

Continue reading...

events.title: Total solar eclipse books out hotels two years in advance in New Zealand city
events.summary:
Dunedin will be the focal point, having last experienced such an eclipse in 1163 – before any humans reached New Zealand

Hotels are booking out two years in advance in the New Zealand city of Dunedin as it prepares for the region’s first total solar eclipse in more than 850 years.

At 4.17pm on 22 July 2028, the moon will pass in front of the sun, casting a roughly 100km-wide shadowy band across parts of southern New Zealand. Everywhere within that band – from tourist hotspot Queenstown in the west to Dunedin city in the east – will be plunged into daytime darkness for 2 minutes and 51 seconds.

Continue reading...

Prosecutors in South Korea have asked a court to sentence the owner of a Michelin-starred restaurant to one year in prison for serving a dessert topped with ants, an insect not approved for consumption under local food safety law.

The request, made on Monday, also sought a 20m won (£10,108) fine for the company that runs the restaurant, Yonhap news agency reported.

events.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
events.summary:
In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat

Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. The Japan Meteorological Agency coined the new designation to warn the public of the heat dangers of days that were once freak weather events but have now become more common.

The central Japan cities of Gujo and Tajimi in Gifu prefecture, along with Toyota in Aichi prefecture, all hit 40C. Gujo’s 40C and Toyota’s 40.1C were all-time highs recorded at their respective weather stations.

Continue reading...

Volkswagen has reported a steep fall in profits and cut its revenue forecast amid a sales slump in China, as the German carmaker pushes through a brutal cost-cutting programme that includes axeing up to 100,000 jobs.

VW said it expects sales to fall by up to 3% this year, a reversal of a previous forecast for a 3% increase on last year’s €321.9bn (£275.3bn), because of a sales slump in the highly competitive Chinese market.

Donald Trump’s unprecedented drive to change election rules, which states legally set, is fueled by his obsession with his 2020 election loss to Joe Biden, which he falsely attributes to voting fraud, coupled with his fear that Democrats will “find a reason to impeach me” if they take control of the House in November’s midterms elections, say former election officials and legal experts.

events.summary:

Prenatal and early life exposure to Pfas mixtures is probably linked to higher levels of intestinal inflammation in children, new first-of-its-kind research shows. Intestinal inflammation can cause irritable bowel diseases, like ulcerative colitis, Crohn’s disease and colorectal cancer.

While Pfas has been linked to irritable bowel disease and intestinal inflammation in adults, the new peer-reviewed Mt Sinai study is the first to look at how the chemicals impact children during a critical developmental phase.

events.title: First Thing: Trump imposes fresh tariffs on UK, EU and dozens of other trading partners
events.summary:
Measures replace 10% global duty and come after supreme court declared many of the earlier tariffs illegal. Plus, how secondhand fashion got so expensive

Good morning.

Donald Trump has imposed a fresh round of tariffs on more than 80 countries to replace a 10% global duty that was due to expire, provoking a wave of criticism and protests from US allies and trading partners.

What is Trump’s justification for the tariffs? The newest tariffs, announced late last night by the US trade representative, Jamieson Greer, would fall under section 301 of the Trade Act of 1974, aimed against countries that engage in forced labor. Trump had said his administration would investigate unfair trading practices to impose permanent tariffs as soon as the February supreme court decision was announced.

How have the affected countries reacted to the news? The ‘forced labour’ rationale for Trump tariffs has been met with bewilderment, with some saying their labor laws are stronger than those in the US.

How are the authorities responding? Last week, Bill Essayli, an assistant US attorney, asked the Environmental Protection Agency to investigate the cluster to determine if there is an environmental cause.

events.summary: Very few Russian deserters are granted asylum in Germany, despite reports of abuse and torture within the Russian military. DW spoke with a young Russian man who fears being sent back to the front lines.

events.title: Russia's largest ballistic assault of war rocks Kyiv
events.summary: Fires broke out across the Ukrainian capital after Russia's latest missile attack, which used the highest number of ballistic missiles of the war so far. Several people have been injured in Kyiv.

events.title: Germany: CDU's Jens Spahn resigns amid surrogacy controversy
events.summary: Surrogacy is not permitted in Germany and the conservative Christian Democratic Union (CDU) opposes its legalization. Spahn and his husband had their child through a surrogate mother in the US.

events.title: Norway: Huge fire destroys 100 homes, sparks evacuations
events.summary: Firefighters from other areas of Norway were drafted in to help tackle the blaze in the southern city of Drammen.

events.title: Reproductive medicine laws in Germany 'shockingly unjust'
events.summary: Infertility affects one in six women in Germany. However, access to reproductive medicine is not available to all. Surrogacy as well as egg donation are banned.

events.title: Why did Jens Spahn's surrogacy spark debate in Germany?
events.summary: Jens Spahn, a key ally of Chancellor Friedrich Merz, is stepping down amid a controversy surrounding his decision to have a child via a surrogate mother. He had opposed legalizing surrogacy in Germany.

events.title: Germany cracks down on money laundering, tax fraud
events.summary: The German government plans to crack down on tax fraud, money laundering and illegally acquired assets with more audits and stiffer penalties.

events.title: Germany's 5,000 heat deaths: What the numbers reveal
events.summary: More than 5,100 people in Germany died from heat-related causes this year until the end of June, the Robert Koch Institute reports. But how can researchers prove that someone died because of soaring temperatures?

events.title: Merz and Macron: A Franco-German week of harmony
events.summary: Friedrich Merz and Emmanuel Macron did everything they could this week to demonstrate a close partnership. This is likely because both fear that the next French president could be nationalist Marine Le Pen.

events.title: Cities are getting smarter about water and other eco wins
events.summary: From flood-resistant infrastructure to new ways of securing drinking water, cities are adapting to the growing climate pressures of a hotter, drier world. Plus, tourists pitching in to help sea turtles.

events.title: How new PM Andy Burnham will change UK's international role
events.summary: From northwest England to the global stage: Andy Burnham becomes Britain's new prime minister on Monday. How will the former mayor of Manchester tackle international affairs?

events.title: The EU climate policy the world copied is under fire — what's at stake?
events.summary: The EU Commission has proposed to weaken Europe's emissions trading system, a global blueprint for pricing carbon and cutting CO2. How effective is the scheme and where does it fall short?

events.title: How AI and satellites help fight wildfires
events.summary: Wildfires are raging across Europe, Canada and beyond. To successfully fight these fires, speed is essential. A German startup uses satellite data and AI to spot fires early.

events.title: Iran's oil supply threat extends beyond Strait of Hormuz
events.summary: Oil and gas producers in the Gulf are seeking alternatives to the Strait of Hormuz as traffic again comes under fire. But is Iran also turning its attention to the pipelines meant to bypass the waterway?

events.title: German tenants need better heat protection
events.summary: Heat waves are becoming longer, hotter and more frequent. Record temperatures have left many tenants struggling. Now Chancellor Friedrich Merz has said the building law needs to be amended.

events.title: Germany massively expands federal police powers
events.summary: The German government has increased the powers of its federal police, especially in the use of AI, surveillance and drone defenses. But experts warn of constitutional challenges.

events.title: Germany may make it illegal to deny Israel's right to exist
events.summary: Germany's upper house of parliament, the Bundesrat, has introduced a bill seeking to ban the questioning of Israel's right to exist. It is now up to the lower house to decide whether to turn it into law.

events.title: US slaps 25% tariffs on Brazil with election looming
events.summary: The US will impose a 25% tariff on most imports from Brazil starting later this month. It's the first action in the Trump administration's new tariff strategy, after the Supreme Court struck down its prior impositions.

events.title: AI: Why Europe is falling behind, and how it can catch up
events.summary: Europe trails the US and China in artificial intelligence. Economist and Nobel laureate Philippe Aghion says better research funding, venture capital and AI innovation can help it catch up.

events.title: Will gold prices extend their record-breaking run?
events.summary: Predictions suggest the price of the precious metal will keep hitting new record highs as investors seek a safe bet in turbulent times. Some forecasts say prices could double within five years.

events.title: East-West border death zone now Germany's biodiversity oasis
events.summary: Many endangered species now live in the area that once formed the border between East and West Germany. Environment Minister Carsten Schneider recently visited the "Green Belt," and DW went along for the ride.

events.title: Could Russia be about to lose its grip on chess leadership?
events.summary: Russians have long pulled the strings when it comes to the governing body of international chess. But now two Germans are vying for FIDE's top job.

events.title: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz makes appeal to AfD voters
events.summary: Germany's chancellor has insisted that his coalition has "regained its footing" — despite dismal poll numbers. Friedrich Merz also cautioned against voting for the far-right AfD party.

events.title: England vs. Argentina: A football rivalry full of history
events.summary: From the Falkland Islands to Diego Maradona and David Beckham, there is so much history between the two 2026 World Cup semifinalists.

events.title: Running dry: How to store more groundwater for dry seasons
events.summary: More frequent weather extremes are leading to water shortages during droughts and flooding during heavy rain. But groundwater storage can help secure water for dry seasons while mitigating extreme rain.

events.title: Why Indonesia's economy is under pressure
events.summary: Southeast Asia's largest economy risks losing its emerging-market status as President Prabowo Subianto's spending plans rattle markets. Can he deliver growth?

events.title: 150 years of the Bayreuth Festival: Confronting a troubled legacy
events.summary: Should the past be confronted — or avoided? Why the Wagner festival's handling of its Nazi-era legacy remains a contentious issue in its 150th anniversary year.

events.title: Germany's Merz receives death threat from Iranian newspaper
events.summary: In retaliation for Khamenei's death, a Tehran daily listed 13 world leaders, including German Chancellor Friedrich Merz, as targets. Politicians voiced concern.

events.title: T. rex 'Gus' sells for record-breaking $50.1 million
events.summary: The Tyrannosaurus rex skeleton surpassed its pre-sale estimate and set a new auction record for a dinosaur fossil.

events.title: Micronutrients are good, a balanced diet is better
events.summary: Chronic illnesses, heart disease, obesity ― we know eating too much of the wrong foods can be unhealthy. But what about "good" foods: Is modern farming diluting micronutrients in our fruit and vegetables?

events.title: Germany's Ahr Valley: Rebuilding better after deadly flood
events.summary: New walls, redesigned bridges and restored floodplains: Towns in a small German valley are testing how they can survive the next extreme flood.

events.title: Germany set to restrict its Freedom of Information Act
events.summary: Critics fear the reforms could weaken government transparency by curbing civil society's access to official information and limiting requests from foreigners.

events.title: World Cup: Balogun red and expansion keep FIFA in spotlight
events.summary: The fallout from Folarin Balogun's red card reversal continues as reports suggest the chairman of FIFA’s disciplinary committee made the decision unilaterally. Meanwhile, the World Cup could be set to expand once more.

events.title: Are German companies leaving the country?
events.summary: Should I stay or should I go? Given Germany's high cost and sluggish growth, some business leaders are mulling relocating, or at least investing abroad. DW has a look at the numbers.

events.title: California's largest lake is turning to dust
events.summary: The Salton Sea is drying up, worsening air pollution for nearby communities. Officials are racing to contain it, but for some families, it's already too late.

events.title: Germany's Ruhr Valley: Cities on brink of financial collapse
events.summary: With tax revenues down and social spending up, more and more cities and towns in the Ruhrgebiet are struggling. Oberhausen is particularly deep in the red.

events.title: Germany news: Neuschwanstein killer fights extradition to US
events.summary: A man who murdered a woman at Neuschwanstein Castle says he fears execution in the US. Germany has been hit by another heat wave, though no records are likely to be broken this time, forecasters say.

events.title: Heat is especially hard on people taking antidepressants
events.summary: High temperatures can increase the risk of dizziness, dehydration, exhaustion and other heat-related problems for people taking antidepressants.

events.title: Germany's richest man takes on Big Tech
events.summary: Supermarket billionaire Dieter Schwarz is investing billions in his cloud company, Schwarz Digits, to rival Google, Microsoft and Amazon.

events.title: Ukraine's Bidnyi 'outraged' by Russia's Olympic return
events.summary: In exclusive comments to DW, Ukraine's sports minister, Matviy Bidnyi, said he is outraged by the IOC opening the door to Russia's return to the Olympics

events.title: When London stank to high heaven
events.summary: In 1858, London sweltered through an unbearable heat wave. The River Thames reeked so badly it left people gasping for air. "The Great Stink" went down in history — and ultimately gave London a modern sewer system.

events.title: Vegan pregnancy tests and other eco wins this week
events.summary: Keeping cool in the heat with a millennia-old tradition in India, a German company making animal-free medical tests, and building from bottles in Mozambique.

events.title: Why the World Cup can be good for your mental health
events.summary: The World Cup can offer more than just entertainment, it can also benefit your mental well-being. DW explains why.

events.title: What's behind Germany's World Cup woes?
events.summary: Germany have endured three straight disappointing World Cups. Experts say the reasons why are connected to football, but also to German society.

events.title: What happens when your fun-loving grandfather — a good husband, an upright citizen — turns out to have been a Nazi?
events.summary: Since millions of NSDAP membership cards have been made available online, many Germans have been surprised to discover that their ancestors weren't always as innocent as family stories had led them to believe.

events.title: Bonnie Tyler, singer of 'Total Eclipse of the Heart,' dies at 75
events.summary: The Welsh singer was best known for the 1980s rock opera hit, along with other songs, including "It's a Heartache."

events.title: Is the Strait of Hormuz still Iran's trump card?
events.summary: Iran's latest attacks show it can easily disrupt shipping, energy markets and draw in Gulf neighbors. DW asks how much leverage Tehran really has over Washington and if its high-risk strategy could backfire.

events.title: Egypt's wrath after World Cup exit: 'The match was rigged'
events.summary: Egypt were left seething by their narrow and dramatic round-of-16 defeat against Argentina. Coach Hossam Hassan accused the referee of favoring the defending World Cup champions.

events.title: Assassin's Creed: Why pop culture is hooked to pirates
events.summary: Pirates have captured our imagination for centuries. With "Assassin's Creed Black Flag Resynced," gamers can now head off on another pirate adventure. How has our concept of pirates evolved with pop culture?

events.title: The Australian tree supercharging global wildfires
events.summary: As Europe experiences another summer of extreme heat and wildfire warnings, one tree imported from Australia is coming under renewed scrutiny across the world: eucalyptus.

events.title: Brain tumors: New vaccine offers patients hope for more time
events.summary: A new therapy offers people with seemingly incurable brain tumors scope for hope: It could slow the progression of the disease and extend patients' lives.

events.title: Conflict, climate change, cash-strapped: Why poverty persists
events.summary: With just under five years left to complete the UN's Sustainable Development Goals, a new report shows what's hampering progress. And what the solutions are.

events.title: Heat-resistant housing: How we need to adapt our homes
events.summary: In Germany, it is time to rethink and rebuild. Many apartments were not designed for heat waves. Fortunately, there are ways to adapt existing buildings to the changing climate — but air conditioning isn't the best.

events.title: Mbappe hits back at racist insults from Paraguay senator
events.summary: A Paraguayan lawmaker has sparked outrage with racist insults directed at footballer Kylian Mbappe. The French FA says it will take legal action, and President Emmanuel Macron has expressed support for the player.

events.title: Could football break away from FIFA and its World Cup?
events.summary: FIFA’s actions at the World Cup, and closeness to Donald Trump, have put the organization under fire. Federations, fans, coaches and players have gone public with complaints, but is there a way out?

events.title: Why Volkswagen's huge workforce became a costly burden
events.summary: The struggling German carmaker plans to cut up to 100,000 jobs from its 630,000-strong workforce. How did VW's headcount get so bloated, and will the cuts be enough to survive the Chinese EV onslaught?

events.title: World Cup 2026: Trump, FIFA and the Balogun controversy
events.summary: One of the few certainties of World Cup football has been rocked after Donald Trump lobbied FIFA to overturn a US red card suspension. The decision has caused outrage but it isn't completely without precedent.

events.title: Fashion against fascism: When trademarks deter neo-Nazis
events.summary: In Germany, where Nazi symbols are banned, activists are using trademark law to fight far-right merchandise.

events.title: Chinese Tianwen-2 space probe reaches asteroid for sampling
events.summary: After a yearlong flight of a billion kilometers, a Chinese space probe is set to take samples from an asteroid. The mission is part of what President Xi Jinping calls the country's "space dream."

events.title: European space telescope Euclid spots oldest quasars yet
events.summary: Think of quasars as time machines. Rare but revealing. They are light from the beginnings of the universe that let astronomers investigate how early galaxies formed.

events.title: Why US train travel lags behind Asia and Europe
events.summary: As flights get pricier and gas prices stay high, more US travelers are turning to trains — and hitting a system built for freight, not people.

events.title: Free electricity in Australia, and the week's other eco wins
events.summary: Germans save an ancient woodland from coal mining, Australia gives away surplus solar power, and how one city became the world's best place for cyclists?

events.title: Far-right AfD threatens eastern Germany's energy transition
events.summary: Germany's far-right AfD wants to revive coal and nuclear power and curb non-EU immigration. Businesses in eastern Germany warn that its policies could harm the economy.

events.title: Sick leave: Germany rising but not the worst in Europe
events.summary: Sick leave in Germany has hit a new record, with workers taking an average of nearly 20 days off per year. As Chancellor Merz cracks down, how does the country's absenteeism compare to other countries?

events.title: The downfall: Julian Nagelsmann out as Germany coach
events.summary: The German Football Associationand national team coach Nagelsmann have parted ways after Germany's poor display at the 2026 World Cup.

events.title: As heat waves loom, scientists wonder how humans will adapt
events.summary: Temperatures are set to soar in the US around the July 4 holiday, a week after Europe struck new records. With experts predicting more intense and more frequent heat waves in the future, can our bodies adapt?

events.title: Is Mexico the 2026 World Cup's best host country?
events.summary: Mexico has fully embraced its role as a World Cup host. Visitors are raving about the atmosphere and say they wish that there were more knockout-stage matches in Mexico as opposed to in the United States.

events.title: Fact check: Racist German fan petition story false
events.summary: It has been claimed that following Germany's World Cup exit, some fans launched a petition seeking to ban "African" and "Muslim" players from the national team — and that DW reported the story. This is not true.

events.title: US declines to renew USMCA trade pact with Mexico, Canada
events.summary: Lengthy negotiations are expected as the Trump administration seeks changes to a trade deal with Mexico and Canada that has streamlined supply chains across North America.

events.title: Bundesliga: Kathleen Krüger takes over as Hamburg's head of sport
events.summary: Hamburg have presented Kathleen Krüger as the club's new board member responsible for sport. She is the first woman to fill such a role at a Bundesliga club on a permanent basis.

events.title: Digital euro: The plan to Trump-proof the EU's economies
events.summary: The EU’s central bank is preparing a digital euro to cut dependence on Visa, Mastercard and Apple Pay. Success hinges on giving consumers an easy-to-use payment system without prompting a flight from bank savings.

events.title: Germany saves forest from mining, US opens land to drilling
events.summary: Germans have won a fight to keep a coal mine out of an ancient forest. In the US, the Trump administration is opening protected lands for drilling and development.

events.title: Portugal's 'golden visa' delays spark legal fight
events.summary: After waiting years for their "golden visas," investors are taking Portugal to court. Tougher citizenship rules have added fuel to the growing dispute.

events.title: Art biennial Manifesta revives Germany's postwar churches
events.summary: Following the destruction of World War II, around 1,000 new churches were built in Germany's Ruhr region. Today, many stand empty, but the contemporary art show Manifesta is giving them a new lease on life.

events.title: Comedy legend Mel Brooks turns 100
events.summary: A parody of Adolf Hitler in the landmark 1968 movie "The Producers" was just the beginning for US actor Mel Brooks. The 100-year-old comedian is still on a roll today.

events.title: Creative climate messaging and other eco wins of the week
events.summary: An artist in Mali and climate scientists in the US are finding new ways to inform the public about the climate crisis.

events.title: Wagner festival reinstates Holocaust memorial event after cancellation
events.summary: The Bayreuth Festival had canceled a planned memorial lecture featuring Jewish author and broadcaster Michel Friedman, drawing sharp criticism.

events.title: Bayer: Why so many Roundup weedkiller lawsuits?
events.summary: Bayer was sued for failing to add cancer warnings to its controversial pesticide Roundup when US regulators opposed adding one. DW asks if the latest Supreme Court win will shield the chemical giant from paying billions.

events.title: Here's what southern Europeans know about surviving heat waves
events.summary: People in countries like Greece, Italy and Spain have long lived with high temperatures. Here's what more northerly countries can learn from them about managing heat waves as they become more common.

events.title: Human rights expert Philippe Sands wins German Peace Prize
events.summary: From advocating for the law of ecocide to representing Palestinian rights, the descendant of Holocaust survivors tirelessly fights against genocide and crimes against humanity.

events.title: How countries live with earthquakes and build resilience
events.summary: Earthquakes are a fact of nature, but living with earthquakes doesn't have to mean living in fear. DW takes a closer look at some countries showing how preparation, innovation and determination can save lives.

events.title: Why Germans don't have air conditioning
events.summary: Unlike the US or parts of Asia, many homes in Germany and northern Europe aren't equipped to deal with extreme heat. But as the world gets hotter, trends are changing.

events.title: Trump to speak at rescheduled White House Correspondents’ Dinner following failed April shooting
events.summary: Nearly three months after a botched shooting cut the annual White House press gala short, US President Donald Trump is expected to speak at a rescheduled dinner held at the Waldorf Astoria hotel Friday. The event will take place under heightened security.

events.title: Giorgia Meloni's Italy: An authoritarian drift? (Part 2)
events.summary: In this edition of Europe Rendezvous, we travel to Italy, one of the European Union's founding members, to examine how the country's democratic institutions have come under increasing pressure. It may be the season of la dolce vita, but life has been far from sweet for migrants, women seeking abortions in parts of Italy, and journalists facing growing political pressure on the media. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni leads a coalition of conservatives and the far right, and as the 2027 elections approach, her government has been accused of seeking greater influence over the judiciary, the media and the electoral system.

events.title: Giorgia Meloni's Italy: An authoritarian drift? (Part 1)
events.summary: In this edition of Europe Rendezvous, we travel to Italy, one of the European Union's founding members, to examine how the country's democratic institutions have come under increasing pressure. It may be the season of la dolce vita, but life has been far from sweet for migrants, women seeking abortions in parts of Italy, and journalists facing growing political pressure on the media. Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni leads a coalition of conservatives and the far right, and as the 2027 elections approach, her government has been accused of seeking greater influence over the judiciary, the media and the electoral system.

events.title: Meloni's Italy: Fortress Europe?
events.summary: Migration was at the heart of Giorgia Meloni's successful 2022 election campaign. Promising a tougher approach to irregular migration helped propel her to power, but what has her government achieved since then? One of its flagship policies was the creation of a controversial migrant processing centre in Albania. Yet the plan has repeatedly run into legal obstacles in Italy, raising questions about whether it can deliver on its promises. At the same time, Meloni has had to balance her hardline rhetoric with economic realities. Faced with labour shortages and pressure from businesses, her government has authorised around half a million additional work visas for the 2026–2029 period. Has Meloni's migration policy proved more pragmatic than her supporters expected? Whatever the answer, it has created political space on her right. One of the most prominent challengers is former army general Roberto Vannacci, who advocates an even tougher approach to immigration and openly calls for the "remigration" of migrants. Luke Brown reports.

events.title: #France: Over 40,000 people evacuated due to #wildfires in the #southwest
events.summary: Over 40,000 people are being evacuated from the very touristic #CapFerret peninsula as forest #fires spread across the French #southwest. More than 10,000 hectares have burned down.

events.title: 'Incredibly dire': Nine months into Gaza ceasefire, conditions 'should have improved by now'
events.summary: Alison Sargent is pleased to welcome Shaina Low, Communications Advisor for the Norwegian Refugee Council's Palestine mission. Despite the reduction in large scale hostilities Low argues that Gaza remains trapped in a state of structural emergency, where humanitarian actors are prevented from moving beyond short term relief because of extensive restrictions on aid, construction materials, fuel, and operational access. She asserts that the central issue is no longer simply the scale of destruction, but the inability to reverse it despite the existence of a ceasefire.

events.title: Giorgia Meloni: The face of Europe's far right?
events.summary: Giorgia Meloni made history in 2022 when she became Italy's first female prime minister. She quickly signalled her ideological affinity with Donald Trump and positioned herself as a bridge between the MAGA movement in the United States and Europe's radical right. She was also the only European leader invited to attend Trump's second inauguration in 2025. Renaud Lefort examines how the relationship between Trump and Meloni has evolved since then, alongside Meloni's changing role within the European Union. While she has sought to strengthen Italy's influence in Brussels, critics argue that she has moderated some of her more hardline positions in order to remain politically influential. The report features an interview with Gianfranco Fini, founder of the post-fascist National Alliance, where Meloni began her political career more than two decades ago. Historian and Italian politics specialist Marc Lazar also assesses Meloni's first three years in office, examining what he describes as authoritarian tendencies in her approach to governing.

events.title: France: Why is the Southwest so vulnerable to wildfires?
events.summary: The Gironde region around Bordeaux is once again battling major wildfires, four years after a devastating blaze swept through the area. As Morgan Ayre explains, a combination of high-risk factors, including vast forests of maritime pine, persistent drought and heavy tourist traffic, has made the region especially vulnerable to fast-moving fires.

events.title: Wildfires hound Tunisia, Algeria and Morocco
events.summary: Deadly forest fires have been raging across Algeria, Morocco and Tunisia for more than a week, as an ongoing heatwave, dry conditions and strong winds fuel the flames and hamper firefighting efforts. Firefighters across the region are struggling to contain the blazes, which have burned vast areas and forced evacuations. Emily Boyle reports.

events.title: Gaza faces ongoing food crisis despite ceasefire
events.summary: More than nine months into the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas, two-thirds of Gaza's population is still facing crisis levels of food insecurity, according to a new UN report released on Thursday. The report warns that recent gains could quickly be reversed unless humanitarian agencies continue to receive sustained access to the enclave, along with adequate funding and stability. For more on the humanitarian situation, we're joined by Shana Low, Communications Adviser for the Norwegian Refugee Council's Palestine mission, speaking to us from Amman, Jordan.

events.title: India extends internet shutdown amid youth protests
events.summary: India has extended a mobile internet shutdown in the capital, New Delhi, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi faces his biggest youth-led challenge since taking office in 2014. Leaders of the youth-led "Cockroach" movement are expected to meet government officials later today in an effort to resolve a standoff over the country's education system. The talks come hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike, amid mounting public anger over exam irregularities and a series of other scandals. Rachael Griffiths reports.

events.title: Six candidates vying to become the next UN secretary after Guterres
events.summary: Six candidates vying to become the next UN secretary-general presented their visions for the organisation on Thursday, as the United Nations faces a financial and confidence crisis ahead of a selection process that will determine its leadership from January 2027. France 24 International affairs editor Kethevane Gorjestani shares further insights on candidates.

events.title: Oil prices surge above $100 a barrel for first time since May
events.summary: Global oil prices have surged once again as tensions in the Middle East continue to escalate. Brent crude has climbed above $100 a barrel for the first time since May. While much of the focus has been on the strategic Strait of Hormuz, shipping through the Red Sea is also coming under growing threat after Yemen's Iran-backed Houthi rebels said they targeted two Saudi oil tankers. The escalation comes as Donald Trump said he was considering a "massive attack" on Iran, prompting Tehran to warn it would hold accountable any country supporting a US strike. Peter O'Brien reports.

events.title: France battles escalating wildfire crisis
events.summary: Following intense wildfires earlier this week, authorities have ordered evacuations across the villages of Lège-Cap-Ferret, with more than 20,000 people already forced to flee. The situation remains critical, as at least 4,800 hectares have been consumed by the flames. Caitlin Brown has more.

events.title: India youth movement agrees to talk but insists protests will continue
events.summary: India has extended a mobile internet shutdown in the capital, New Delhi, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi faces his biggest youth-led challenge since taking office in 2014. Leaders of the youth-led "Cockroach" movement are due to meet government officials later today in an effort to resolve a standoff over the country's education system. The talks come hours after activist Sonam Wangchuk ended a 26-day hunger strike, amid growing public anger over a series of exam irregularities and other scandals. France 24 correspondent Sarah Jacob reports from New Delhi.

events.title: Spain declares wildfire emergency, 10,000 evacuated near Madrid
events.summary: The Spanish government ⁠has declared a national emergency after a series of wildfires in the Madrid region and in the province of Avila, near the capital, late on Thursday. The measure aims to speed up the deployment of resources to fight the blazes, which have forced some 10,000 people in the region to evacuate. According to Spanish authorities, "not enough has been done" by the government to contain and prevent these fires, which are no longer a temporary emergency. France 24 correspondent Sarah Morris reports the latest from Madrid.

events.title: France: Two major wildfires hit the southwest forcing tourists to evacuate
events.summary: Deadly wildfires are sweeping across western Europe, fueled by scorching temperatures and strong winds, as climate change drives fires to spread faster and burn more intensely. The total area scorched in EU countries this year is already the second largest on record. In France, where three heatwaves have hit since May, wildfires in the southwest have forced more than 20,000 people to evacuate. Around 800 firefighters, backed by hundreds of gendarmes and soldiers, are battling the flames about 50 kilometres west of Bordeaux, as the crisis unfolds during the peak summer holiday season. Among those evacuated are hundreds of tourists who had come to spend their holidays in the area. One of them, Dutch tourist Barry Pollack, joined us to tell us more.

events.title: Middle East: Oil surges as Gulf tensions escalate
events.summary: Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthi rebels say they attacked two Saudi oil tankers in the Red Sea on Thursday, marking their first reported strike on a vessel since announcing a blockade of Saudi-linked shipping through the Bab el-Mandeb Strait earlier this week. The Houthis say the move is retaliation for Saudi Arabia’s blockade on Yemen and a recent strike on Sanaa’s international airport. The escalation adds further pressure to global shipping as the Strait of Hormuz remains closed and Brent crude prices hit their highest level since May. Morgan Ayre and Charlotte Lam explain why the Bab el-Mandeb Strait matters. Morgan Ayre and Charlotte Lam report.

events.title: Four Palestinians and one Israeli killed in occupied West Bank shooting
events.summary: Four Palestinians and an Israeli were killed in a shooting in the Israeli-occupied West Bank Friday, Israeli and Palestinian officials said. The Israeli military said it was preparing to launch an "extensive" operation into the occupied Palestinian territory in response to the violence.

events.title: New Caledonia: A deeply divided territory
events.summary: Two years after the riots that shook New Caledonia in 2024, FRANCE 24 returns to a French overseas territory still grappling with the consequences of one of the darkest chapters in its recent history. Travelling from the Kanak independence stronghold of Saint-Louis to Mont-Dore, Nouméa and Bourail, our reporters meet the people living with the lasting scars of the violence.

events.title: US hits partners with new tariffs over forcer labour claims
events.summary: Starting this Friday, the United States will impose new tariffs on imports from 60 trading partners, covering 99 percent of all goods entering the country. The new measures replace the temporary 10 percent global tariff introduced 150 days ago after the Supreme Court struck down President Donald Trump's previous sweeping tariff plan. The administration says the latest tariffs are aimed at addressing concerns over goods produced with forced labour. Peter O'Brien reports.

events.title: Spain: Thousands evacuate as heatwave fuels blazes near Madrid
events.summary:
Thousands of people were forced to flee deadly wildfires that continued to sweep through parts of France, Spain and Italy on Friday.

Hundreds of firefighters backed by water-bombing planes battled the blazes fanned by scorching temperatures and strong winds. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

events.title: 🔴 Live: Thousands forced to flee as deadly wildfires rage in parts of France, Italy and Spain
Live: More than 40,000 evacuated in southwestern France as wildfire remains out of control
events.summary: More than 40,000 were evacuated from southwestern France over the past two days as wildfires continued to sweep through the region and were not yet under control, the local prefect said Friday. Spain’s government declared a national emergency and hundreds of firefighters backed by water-bombing planes battled the blazes fanned by scorching temperatures and strong winds. Follow our liveblog for the latest developments.

events.title: 'Extremely significant' car bomb intercepted near Irish border

events.title: An accident took his legs. He adapted his motorcycle and rides on in Pakistan
events.summary:
A young man in Pakistan’s capital lost both legs in a road accident five years ago, but he refused to let that define his future.

Today, 28-year-old Hamza Ali delivers meals across Islamabad, riding a modified motorcycle fitted with hand-operated controls.

Ali appeared cheerful under the scorching sun as he told The Associated Press that he had worked for a delivery platform before the accident. Then a bus he was riding collided with a truck.

“After that accident, the first six months of my life were extremely difficult,” he said. “Then I told myself, ‘Ali, you have to do something on your own.’”

After nearly three years of job hunting in his hometown of Multan, where employers turned him away because of his disability, Ali moved to Islamabad in 2023 on a friend’s advice that the capital’s wider roads make it easier to ride a modified motorcycle.
